header.title: Experience
header.description: Career timeline displayed like a flight log.

description: Built certification-ready finite element models for B777-9 interior ceiling panel assemblies using HyperMesh, including geometry cleanup, midsurface extraction, shell meshing, beam modeling, and structural connections.

Performed stress analysis, load path evaluation, and Margin of Safety (MoS) assessments for multiple interior commodities using MSC Nastran, Patran, and HyperView, supporting FAA and EASA certification activities.

Developed engineering automation tools in Microsoft Excel, including a section analysis calculator for critical node identification and beam cross-section MoS calculations, reducing manual engineering effort.
Created a lap shear joint calculator for bonded joints, enabling faster structural substantiation and design verification.
Worked with Boeing structural design standards, material specifications, and certification documentation while collaborating with experienced stress engineers.
Conducted material characterization of low-density EPDM ablative insulation for Solid Rocket Motors used in aerospace propulsion applications.

Performed mechanical, thermal, and chemical testing including density, Shore A hardness, tensile strength, elongation, rheometry, thermal conductivity, FTIR, glass transition temperature (Tg), coefficient of thermal expansion (CTE), and crosslink density.
Analyzed experimental results to evaluate insulation performance under high-temperature operating conditions for rocket motor applications.
Collaborated with scientists and researchers to support the development and validation of advanced thermal protection materials.
